ideas.title: DateWeave
ideas.one_liner: Discover surprising connections between your dates and the moments that shaped the world.
ideas.key_insight: Existing birthday apps focus on individual trivia. The relational cross-date magic — connecting your dates to people and history — is the unique angle. No competitor does this.
ideas.notes: Built as first test of the Claude → PushCraft → Vercel pipeline. Has forward-looking engine, custom birthday picker, life events support. Quick-scored: fun but not life-changing, hard to monetize. Serves its purpose as pipeline proof-of-concept. Sustainability 78%: pure client-side, no API deps, nothing to break.
ideas.hero_description: Enter your birthday, add the people who matter, pick historical eras — and DateWeave weaves them together into surprising connections you never noticed.

ideas.title: PushCraft
ideas.one_liner: Push code from Claude to GitHub from your phone. No terminal required.
ideas.key_insight: GitHub API supports CORS for authenticated requests — no backend needed. The copy-paste bridge from Claude to PushCraft is the workflow ceiling. Nothing else does Claude → GitHub from phone.
ideas.notes: v4: multi-file atomic commit, quick push, token persistence (sessionStorage), scaffold templates (Vite + Expo). Self-bootstrapping — upgrades itself. Quick-scored: genuine gap in tooling, niche reach limits business score. Sustainability 69%: GitHub API CORS/token behavior is the main risk.
ideas.hero_description: The missing bridge between AI-generated code and production deployment. Scaffold, stage, push — all from your phone.

ideas.title: RepairShield
ideas.one_liner: Home repair triage for low-income homeowners and the nonprofits that help them.
ideas.key_insight: Habitat for Humanity Grand Traverse Region and Northwest Michigan Community Action Agency identified as local launch partners.
ideas.notes: Scored Unicorn. Prioritizes repairs, connects homeowners with assistance programs, helps nonprofits allocate limited budgets. Highest combined score in the vault. Sustainability 44%: assistance program data decays fast, ongoing support burden expected.
ideas.hero_description: Prioritizes which repairs matter most, connects homeowners with the right assistance programs, and helps nonprofits allocate limited repair budgets effectively.

ideas.title: Crisis Cascade Navigator
ideas.one_liner: Jurisdiction-aware action sequencer for people facing overlapping crises.
ideas.key_insight: When you lose your job AND face eviction AND need medical care, the order you act in matters. Static-first architecture (YAML → HTML) means $0/month hosting.
ideas.notes: Highest impact score in the vault. Architecture designed: YAML crisis graphs, ZIP-code lookup, static site on Cloudflare. MVP estimated 8 weeks for two people. Starting point: eviction defense in TX, CA, NY. Process Navigator could be the platform layer. Sustainability 67%: hosting is simple but legal/jurisdictional content decays and stale info could harm someone.
ideas.hero_description: When you lose your job AND face eviction AND need medical care — the order you act in matters. This tool sequences the right moves for your specific jurisdiction.

ideas.title: Shelf Life
ideas.one_liner: Predict which retail items will become future collectibles using anomaly detection.
ideas.key_insight: Manufacturing errors, rapid branding changes, recalls, and discontinuations all leave digital footprints in public data feeds before the collectibles market prices them in.
ideas.notes: Quick-scored: interesting concept but unclear demand signal. Subscription model is plausible but unproven. CPSC/FDA recall APIs, news scraping for rebrands/discontinuations. Rule-based scoring for MVP, ML later. Sustainability 37%: heavy API dependencies, content decays daily, many breakage points.
ideas.hero_description: Watches for manufacturing errors, rapid branding changes, recalls, and cultural moments that turn ordinary products into tomorrow's treasures.

ideas.title: TC Relocation Guide
ideas.one_liner: Hyperlocal housing intelligence for Traverse City that Zillow doesn't provide.
ideas.key_insight: General home comparison loses to Zillow. TC-specific buyer intelligence — seasonal pricing, Airbnb saturation, micro-market differences — is the gap.
ideas.notes: Personal tool for summer 2026 move. 9 neighborhood profiles, seasonal pricing charts, buyer strategy. Quick-scored: high personal utility, audience of ~1. Not a business — and that's fine. Sustainability 78%: static content, no APIs, but neighborhood data will age after the move.

ideas.title: Map Mosaic
ideas.one_liner: Paint on a world map canvas using historical, artistic, and data maps instead of paint.
ideas.key_insight: Leaflet.js or Mapbox GL JS recommended. Map projection alignment is the main challenge. Data licensing flagged as key risk.
ideas.notes: Quick-scored: creative and novel but unclear demand. Public domain maps from David Rumsey Collection and Library of Congress. Moderate difficulty — few weekends for prototype, 2-3 months polished. Sustainability 57%: map tile dependency and projection alignment are breakage risks.

ideas.title: Dice Party Game
ideas.one_liner: A party guessing game with 12 dice that constrain how you give clues — thousands of possible combinations.
ideas.key_insight: 12 dice covering communication mode, word restrictions, perspective, category limits, format, emotional tone, team rules, and scoring twists. Thousands of combos.
ideas.notes: Physical game concept — outside the Claude-first pipeline. Quick-scored: proven market (people pay $20-40 for party games) but manufacturing/distribution is a different beast. Accessibility flagged as concern. Sustainability 70%: no dependencies or decay (physical product), but hosting simplicity is N/A — scored low on that dimension since it's not a web deploy.

ideas.title: Storyweaver
ideas.one_liner: AI-powered interactive fiction and collaborative storytelling platform.
ideas.key_insight: The interactive fiction space is extremely crowded (Talefy, NovelistAI, Character.AI Stories, Sudowrite, NovelCrafter, Rosebud). Differentiation is the key question.
ideas.notes: Deployed with git repo at github.com/fairbay/story. Quick-scored: crowded space drags gap score down hard. Unless there's a unique angle the competitors miss, this is a personal/creative project rather than a business. Sustainability 50%: Anthropic API dependency is the biggest risk — one pricing or CORS change breaks it.
ideas.hero_description: AI-powered interactive fiction with procedural art, voice input, and 16 genre engines. Pick a genre, set preferences, and the story adapts to you.

ideas.title: Shadowrun Story App
ideas.one_liner: Interactive narrative app set in the Shadowrun universe.
ideas.key_insight: IP licensing is a dealbreaker until resolved. Shadowrun IP ownership and licensing terms must be investigated before scoring is meaningful.
ideas.notes: Not scored — IP question must be answered first. If licensing is prohibitive, pivot to original cyberpunk setting using Storyweaver as the platform. Sustainability 39%: AI API dependency + IP licensing risk compound badly.

ideas.title: Arboreal Sentience (Sci-Fi Worldbuild)
ideas.one_liner: A story set on a planet where trees evolved to human-level intelligence before any other species.
ideas.key_insight: Five evolutionary pathways developed (Root Network, Long Burn, Canopy War, Pollinator Collapse, Fire Thinkers), each producing distinct societal values. Scientifically plausible.
ideas.notes: Creative writing project, not a product — scoring framework doesn't apply. Five paths to sentience designed. Classic dramatic structure mapped onto alien civilization. Ongoing worldbuilding. Sustainability 93%: pure text, zero dependencies, nothing decays or breaks.

ideas.title: Parking Incentive Inversion
ideas.one_liner: Credit patients who arrive early for appointments to reduce schedule cascade failures.
ideas.key_insight: Interesting concept but depends on institutional partnerships that are hard to get. IDF drafted for Cleveland Clinic.
ideas.notes: Formalized earlier calibration score of 22/54. Invention Disclosure Form drafted. Wariness about long-term financial relationships with large institutions. Requires parking system + scheduling system integration. Correctly parked.

ideas.title: Bluetooth Busy Light
ideas.one_liner: Office availability indicator with separate controller and remote light.
ideas.key_insight: Luxafor Switch Pro is this exact product, shipped and on its second generation. $5-10 puck lights with RF remotes solve the simplified version.
ideas.notes: Pass. Clinical room status indicator (Pivot C) was the only angle with whitespace, but would require hallway conversations with clinical staff to validate.

ideas.title: The Unsolvable Conversations
ideas.one_liner: An AI companion designed to hold philosophical questions open rather than resolve them.
ideas.key_insight: Most AI products try to answer questions — this one deliberately doesn't. Genuinely distinctive angle, but the philosophical AI companion space has growing competition (Hypatia, Pocket Philosopher, Clio). None hold questions open the way this does.
ideas.notes: Quick-scored: impact close to 56% threshold but not there. The 'hold questions open' angle is genuinely novel — every competitor tries to resolve. Monetization is the weak link. Could work as a premium meditative/reflective experience. Sustainability 50%: AI API dependency tanks it.

ideas.title: Process Navigator
ideas.one_liner: Interactive branching flow editor for navigating real-world processes, with community-created content.
ideas.key_insight: Twine is the closest spiritual ancestor but designed for stories, not processes. Decision tree tools (VisiRule, Miro) are for businesses, not people navigating real-world bureaucracy. Gap: no tool combines interactive branching with community content for everyday process navigation.
ideas.notes: Process Navigator v2 built as React artifact (interactive flow editor, JSON import/export). Could be the platform layer for Crisis Cascade Navigator. Quick-scored: strong impact from broad reach + meaningful depth. Business score held back by monetization uncertainty. Sustainability 67%: no API deps but community content needs moderation and process data can decay.
ideas.hero_description: Visual flow editor for building and sharing step-by-step processes. Import, export, and navigate complex workflows with branching logic.

ideas.title: The Grove
ideas.one_liner: Nature-themed portfolio site showing ideas at every stage of growth.
ideas.key_insight: Each idea's maturity depicted as a stage of plant growth — seed to fruit-bearing tree. First-person walk-through with perspective projection. Score-driven flowers (impact) and fruit (business).
ideas.notes: Portfolio site — scoring framework doesn't apply (infrastructure, not product). Reads from projects.json for easy PushCraft updates. Vercel deploy + AWS custom domain at bayleemiller.org. Active development: perspective walk, score-driven plant features, stage-scaled heights. Sustainability 83%: pure client-side React, reads from static JSON, only breakage risk is Vercel config or vault schema changes.

ideas.title: ShadowScale
ideas.one_liner: Tactical roguelike heist game in a cyberpunk-fantasy noir setting.
ideas.key_insight: Pure client-side browser game with 2D tactical grid combat. No AI API dependencies. Ship & Shelve track — high sustainability means build once, leave running.
ideas.notes: Ship & Shelve track. Best version: pure client-side, deploy to Vercel + itch.io.

ideas.title: Traffic-Aware Road Repair Scheduler
ideas.one_liner: Optimize road repair scheduling using traffic pattern data to minimize disruption.
ideas.key_insight: Best pivot was TMP Generator (Impact 60%, Business 58%) but effort too high for scores.
ideas.notes: Killed. Scores not high enough to justify build effort even with best pivot.

ideas.title: Variant Forge
ideas.one_liner: Generate image variants using Gemini and Imagen 4. Paste an image, get styled alternatives.
ideas.key_insight: First tool built on Gemini/Imagen 4 API. Used to generate Grove plant images.
ideas.notes: Infrastructure tool for the pipeline. Quick-built, serves its purpose. Sustainability 57%: Gemini API dependency is the core risk.

ideas.title: Neon Sorcery RPG
ideas.one_liner: Cyberpunk-fantasy RPG with six-episode season structure, twist-based combat, and procedural mercenaries.
ideas.key_insight: Single React component/PWA. 7 metatypes, 7 classes, 6 backgrounds. Meat Market with 100 seeded mercenaries across 4 tiers.
ideas.notes: Deployed to Netlify. Key lesson: always add explicit React import; drag-and-drop Netlify deployment can serve cached versions. Sustainability 87%: pure client-side, seeded RNG, zero external deps.

ideas.title: Colored Pencil Bandolier
ideas.one_liner: Wearable colored pencil organizer for artists.
ideas.key_insight: Scores too low across both lenses. Existing products cover the space.
ideas.notes: Killed. 37%/35% — clear pass.
